old_claim.note: Encyclopedic Compendiums & Knowledge Organization provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because scientific journals are scoped to periodical publication of scientific communications, not encyclopedia-style knowledge compendia.

source_shape.source_locator: Royal Society history of Philosophical Transactions and its 1665 periodical publication model.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Royal Society source frames Philosophical Transactions as a 1665 scientific periodical, not as a product of encyclopedic compendiums.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports print-enabled periodical scientific communication; it does not support encyclopedic knowledge organization as a direct prerequisite for journals.

ontology_before: The graph conflated adjacent knowledge-organization forms by making encyclopedic compendiums an enabling dependency for journals.
ontology_after: Scientific journals keep print and scientific-method/correspondence context while encyclopedic compendiums remain a separate knowledge-organization lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-random-sample-quality-fixes keeps this direct edge absent.
would_reject_if: The scientific_journals node were narrowed to encyclopedia-like serial compendia rather than periodical journals.
A source showed early scientific journals required encyclopedic compendium institutions or formats.
A later node split introduced a narrower indexing or abstracting technology that should depend on compendiums.
short_reason: Scientific journals are periodical communication infrastructure, not dependent on encyclopedic compendiums.
